name: custom-formatter
description: Format files using arbitrary shell commands per language, integrated with format-on-save.

customFormatter.commands.markdownDescription: Map of VS Code language id to formatter command. Available template variables: `${file}`, `${fileName}`, `${fileDirname}`, `${fileExtname}`, `${fileBasenameNoExtension}`, `${workspaceFolder}`.

command.description: Shell command to run. When useStdin is false, ${file} is replaced with a temp file path that the command should modify in place.

useStdin.description: If true, buffer contents are piped to the command's stdin and stdout is used as the formatted result. If false, the file is modified in place via ${file}.

cwd.description: Working directory for the command. Supports the same template variables. Defaults to the file's directory.

customFormatter.autoRegister.description: Automatically set this extension as editor.defaultFormatter for configured languages so you only need to declare them in customFormatter.commands.

customFormatter.autoRegisterTarget.description: Where to write the auto-registered defaultFormatter setting.

commands.title: Custom Formatter: Sync Default Formatter Associations
